entries.label: Lifetime concept
entries.note: Lifetimes ensure references are always valid; compiler tracks how long data lives

entries.label: Valid reference
entries.code:
let x = 5;     // x lives long enough
let r = &x;    // r borrows x
println!("{r}"); // OK: x is still alive
entries.note: Reference is valid as long as the borrowed data outlives the reference

entries.label: Why annotations needed
entries.note: Annotations describe relationships; they don't change how long values live

entries.label: Basic annotation
entries.note: Return value lives at least as long as input; 'a is a generic lifetime parameter

entries.label: Multiple inputs, same lifetime
entries.note: Both inputs and output share lifetime 'a; result valid while both inputs valid

entries.label: Multiple lifetimes
entries.note: Different lifetime parameters when inputs have independent lifetimes

entries.label: One input determines output
entries.note: Only annotate the lifetime relationships that actually exist

entries.label: Returning owned data (no lifetime needed)
entries.note: Returning owned data needs no lifetime; the caller owns the result

entries.label: Cannot return local reference
entries.note: Cannot return a reference to a value created inside the function

entries.label: Struct holding reference
entries.note: Struct cannot outlive the reference it holds; 'a ties struct lifetime to data

entries.label: Multiple references
entries.note: Different lifetime parameters when references may have different lifetimes

entries.label: Methods on structs with lifetimes
entries.note: impl block must declare lifetime parameters; elision often applies to methods

entries.label: Struct with owned and borrowed
entries.note: Mix owned and borrowed fields; only borrowed fields need lifetime parameters

entries.label: Enum with lifetime
entries.note: Enum variants with references also need lifetime annotations

entries.label: Rule 1: Each input gets its own
entries.note: Each reference parameter gets its own lifetime parameter

entries.label: Rule 2: Single input = output
entries.note: If there's exactly one input lifetime, it's assigned to all outputs

entries.label: Rule 3: &self determines output
entries.note: In methods, the lifetime of &self is assigned to all output lifetimes

entries.label: When elision fails
entries.note: If rules don't resolve all output lifetimes, you must annotate manually

entries.label: Common elided patterns
entries.note: Most simple functions and methods don't need explicit lifetimes

entries.label: String literals
entries.note: All string literals have 'static lifetime; they're embedded in the binary

entries.label: Static variables
entries.note: static variables live for the entire program; have 'static lifetime

entries.label: 'static in trait bounds
entries.note: T: 'static means T contains no non-static references; can live forever if needed

entries.label: Owned types are 'static
entries.note: 'static doesn't mean it lives forever; it means it CAN live forever (no borrows)

entries.label: Box::leak for 'static
entries.code:
let s: &'static str = Box::leak(String::from("leaked").into_boxed_str());
// s is now 'static; memory is intentionally leaked
entries.note: Convert owned data to 'static reference by leaking; use sparingly

entries.label: 'static misconception
entries.note: T: 'static is about what T borrows, not about how long a specific instance lives

entries.label: Longer outlives shorter
entries.note: 'b: 'a means 'b outlives 'a; y lives at least as long as x

entries.label: Struct with subtyping
entries.note: 'alloc: 'input ensures the arena outlives the input

entries.label: Covariance
entries.note: A longer lifetime can always be used where a shorter one is expected

entries.label: for<'a> syntax
entries.note: for<'a> means the function works for ANY lifetime; not tied to a specific one

entries.label: Common in closures
entries.note: HRTB needed when the closure must work with references of varying lifetimes

entries.label: Implicit HRTB
entries.note: Fn(&str) -> &str implicitly uses for<'a>; you rarely need to write it explicitly

entries.label: With trait objects
entries.code:
let f: Box<dyn for<'a> Fn(&'a str) -> &'a str> = Box::new(|s| s);
let result = f("hello");
entries.note: HRTB works with trait objects for maximum flexibility

entries.label: Split borrow
entries.note: Borrowing different fields is fine; Rust tracks borrows per-field

entries.label: Lifetime bound on generic
entries.note: T: 'a means T does not contain references shorter than 'a

entries.label: Anonymous lifetime '_
entries.note: '_ tells compiler to infer the lifetime; reduces annotation noise

entries.label: Lifetime in type aliases
entries.note: Type aliases can include lifetime parameters
